title: Buildah supports selective layer (rootfs.diff_ids) squashing with intermediate image retention in multistage builds
body:
Hello, I’m currently designing functionality for our internal build and release tool **Konflux**, which relies heavily on **Buildah** for container image building. As part of this work, I’ve encountered a potential limitation in Buildah that I’d like to discuss. I'm also prepared to contribute an implementation if we can agree on the best approach. ## Problem In Konflux, we need to access content from intermediate images (e.g., packages, binaries) after the build process of multistage-build images. The only way I’m currently aware of to preserve these intermediate images is by using the `--layers` flag within `buildah build`. This works well for retaining intermediate layers, which we can then inspect post-build. However, enabling `--layers` changes the structure of the final image’s metadata. Specifically: - When using `--layers`, the `rootfs.diff_ids` of the final image includes **all intermediate layers** (undesired). - When **not** using `--layers`, the final image contains only **two layers** in `rootfs.diff_ids` (desired): - One for the base image. - One for the squashed final image layers (I assume). But of course, **no intermediate images** are stored (undesired). - In both cases, with or without `--layers`, the final image preserves the **full history** (also desired) containing parent base image and final image history. - Using `--squash` alongside `--layers` does not achieve the desired result either. This ends up **fully squashing everything** (including the parent base image), which removes necessary layering details (parent base image layer + all other squashed). In addition to that, **history is capturing only the last layer of the final image**, but we would like to preserve full history as it is produced without squash. ## What I cannot do - Unfortunately, performing two separate builds (one with `--layers` for analysis and one without for the final image) is **not viable** for performance and pipeline simplicity reasons. - I cannot use external tools like **docker-squash**, as they are heavily dependent on Docker and we want to certainly avoid that. ## Desired Outcome I’m looking for a way to: - Preserve intermediate images for inspection. - Have only **two layers** in the final image’s `rootfs.diff_ids` (parent base image layer + squashed final image layers). - Retain history metadata for the final image (parent base image and final image history) ## Example Containerfile && `buildah build` examples and outcomes ``` FROM docker.io/library/golang:1.21-bullseye AS builder WORKDIR /app RUN echo 'package main; import "fmt"; func main() { fmt.Println("Hello from Go!") }' > main.go && \ go build -o hello main.go FROM registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9/ubi:latest RUN yum install python3-pip -y RUN pip3 install semver==2.13.0 COPY --from=builder /app/hello /usr/local/bin/hello CMD ["echo"] ``` | Command | Images Created
(buildah images)                           | `rootfs.diff_ids` (simplified) | History Retained | Drawbacks | |--------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------| | `buildah build`
`--layers`
`-t final-image:0.1` | - `final-image:0.1`
- **intermediate**: ``
- base: `ubi9`
- builder: `golang:1.21` | - `sha256:2cef...` (base image layer)
- `sha256:f600...` (yum install)
- `sha256:153a...` (pip install)
- `sha256:43cd...` (COPY hello) | ✅ Yes | ❗ Too many `diff_ids`
in final image – every
instruction becomes a layer | | `buildah build`
`-t final-image:0.1` | - `final-image:0.1`
- base: `ubi9`
- builder: `golang:1.21` | - `sha256:2cef...` (base image layer)
- `sha256:92b3...` (squashed
final image layers) | ✅ Yes | ❗ Intermediate image is not
retained – content from stages is lost | | `buildah build`
`--squash --layers`
`-t final-image:0.1` | - `final-image:0.1`
- **intermediate**: ``
- base: `ubi9`
- builder: `golang:1.21` | - `sha256:92b3...` (fully
squashed: parent base image + all final image layers) | ❌ No
(partial) | ❗ All layers including
parent base image are squashed
❗ History is mostly lost | | `buildah build`
`--new-arg(s)`
`-t final-image:0.1` | - `final-image:0.1`
- **intermediate**: ``
- base: `ubi9`
- builder: `golang:1.21` | - `sha256:2cef...` (base image layer)
- `sha256:92b3...` (squashed final image layers) | ✅ Yes | ✅ Desired behavior – parent base image + final image layers,
preserved history, accessible intermediate stages | ## Potential Proposed Solution Based on current functionality, I believe we could introduce a new **alternative flag: e.g., `--layers-stages-only`** which would store intermediate stages as images, but squashes all filesystem changes of the final image layer chain, effectively yielding the same layer structure as a `buildah build` without `--layers` (parent base image sha + sha containing all other changes from final image manifested in `rootfs.diff_ids`). Of course, I don't insist on the name of the argument, this is just an example, up for discussion. ## Questions - Did I miss anything? Is there currently any supported combination of flags in Buildah that would achieve this behavior? - Would such functionality (separating intermediate stage storage from final image layering) be considered a reasonable feature to add? If so, I’d be happy to submit a patch or collaborate on an implementation aligned with Buildah's design philosophy. --- Thank you for your time and consideration. I’d appreciate any guidance or thoughts on how best to proceed.

comments.body: I suppose _only_ preserving the stage images wouldn’t be sufficient, the build would need to write their IDs to a file, or something like that, for all of this to be reasonable in any environment that is not just a single isolated VM?
I think this is possible and I agree with @mtrmac there needs to be someway to identify what intermediate images were added to store as part of this build only so users can use them later.
@ezopezo One question out of curiosity I have is, how are you using intermediate images later on ? As part of cache ? or directly mounting them ?

Mounting and accessing the content (binaries/packages, etc.) was one of the approaches we considered. However, to reduce resource consumption and improve safety, we decided to use other method: we create a scratch image and use buildah copy to transfer the desired content from an intermediate or builder parent image into created scratch. This resulting scratch image is then stored and analyzed using Syft. 
Just to clarify: the desired content for us is any content that has been COPY-ied from builder stages to final image (paths are parsed from Containerfile) - we ultimately need to identify the content and get a SBOM of it by syft. For reliable identification we need to search it in builder parents or intermediate images, because at the time when this content is COPY-ied to final image, the information what content it actually is, is already lost; `COPY --from=builder /path/with/content .`
BTW Buildah has some native `--sbom`… features, just to make sure they were considered.
To me this feature idea looks good and I think it will be nice for buildah to have this, @nalind WDYT ?

@mtrmac Thanks for pointing this out! Looking briefly on what buildah is currently offering I am afraid that this is not what we are looking for. Moreover, the current existing codebase is heavily dependent on syft and other internal customized SBOM tool, I am afraid that additional tooling manipulating/generating the SBOM might be a problem with regard to potential formatting differences :/

The final instruction for a stage is committed when the stage is used as the base for a later stage.  Otherwise, it isn't needed (references to it via `COPY --from` or `RUN --mount` can be supplied using that stage's final rootfs, which isn't cleaned up until the build finishes), so the effort is skipped.  If that image is committed, it gets removed at the end of the build.

If I understand you right, in the example above, the desired outcome is to end up with

* the base image for the first stage
* the image committed at the end of the first stage
* the base image for the second stage (if it's not the image committed at the end of the first stage, which it isn't in your example), and
* the final image from the second stage,
 
and that would entail

* always committing an image at the end of a stage
* not removing those images when the overall build succeeds

which would be pretty straightforward to add controls for, along the lines of the `--rm` and `--force-rm` flags.  Do I understand correctly?

Being able to find them later, as noted above, becomes the next problem.  My first guess there would be a flag that takes a stage number or nickname, a separator, and a filename, and which can be specified multiple times, along the lines of `--build-arg` or `--label`.

If the rootfs content was enough, and they didn't need to be images with history (as an aside, I wouldn't want to depend on the "created_by" field in history entries, which we tweak for cache evaluation reasons), I'd have suggested extending `--output` to be able to specify which stage to use for generating a given `--output` value, since the caller can directly specify where to write things.  Maybe we'll do that another time.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@nalind Sorry for longer silence I am getting back to this.
> * always committing an image at the end of a stage
> * not removing those images when the overall build succeeds

Yes this is exactly what I need - as it is happening with `--layers` option.

> ...which would be pretty straightforward to add controls for, along the lines of the --rm and --force-rm flags. Do I understand correctly?

Yes (if _I_ understood your point correctly) implementing opposite of the --rm command would be exactly what I need (citing buildah man pages; "`--rm` - remove intermediate containers after a successful build (default true)").
Just adding the note - I intuitively tried this (intentionally with no `--layers` option): `buildah build --rm=false -t image .` but I got an error `Error: 'rm' and 'force-rm' can only be set with either 'layers' or 'no-cache'` and I assuming there needs to be a some reason for that and we need to introduce a new arg with similar behavior as `--rm=false` - or I can just simply lift this limitation?

> Being able to find them later, as noted above, becomes the next problem. My first guess there would be a flag that takes a stage number or nickname, a separator, and a filename, and which can be specified multiple times, along the lines of --build-arg or --label.

Well I already have an approach for that. I am not saying it is best approach but I am working with what I have. Intermediate images (assuming those have both REPOSITORY and TAG  set as <none> in `buildah images` output) are storing pullspec of its own base images - I am obtaining this pullspecs with dockerfile-json from Containerfile, doing `buildah inspect` of those intermediate images and mapping pullspecs there and reconstructing the stages by this way (also taking into account that multiple stages can have same base image). 
Something like argument that would take as parameter stage alias or number and path where builder should be dropped would be definitely better, but I can workaround that if it would be hard to implement. 

> If the rootfs content was enough...

Yes the plain rootfs might be a sufficient content for us but from your message I got an impression that `--rm=false`-like argument would be a more feasible approach right?

> [@nalind](https://github.com/nalind) Sorry for longer silence I am getting back to this.
> 
> > * always committing an image at the end of a stage
> > * not removing those images when the overall build succeeds
> 
> Yes this is exactly what I need - as it is happening with `--layers` option.

I don't think we currently commit at the end of a stage if it isn't going to be used as the base for a later stage, but my understanding is that you'd want that to happen.

> > ...which would be pretty straightforward to add controls for, along the lines of the --rm and --force-rm flags. Do I understand correctly?
> 
> Yes (if _I_ understood your point correctly) implementing opposite of the --rm command would be exactly what I need (citing buildah man pages; "`--rm` - remove intermediate containers after a successful build (default true)"). Just adding the note - I intuitively tried this (intentionally with no `--layers` option): `buildah build --rm=false -t image .` but I got an error `Error: 'rm' and 'force-rm' can only be set with either 'layers' or 'no-cache'` and I assuming there needs to be a some reason for that and we need to introduce a new arg with similar behavior as `--rm=false` - or I can just simply lift this limitation?

I'm not entirely sure why we kept rejecting those flags after we introduced multi-stage builds, since they apply to images we commit at the end of build stages.  The logic that removes images committed for stages in multi-stage builds consults internal flags that those CLI flags control, so you're right, we wouldn't need to introduce new flags for controlling whether or not they get removed.

> > Being able to find them later, as noted above, becomes the next problem. My first guess there would be a flag that takes a stage number or nickname, a separator, and a filename, and which can be specified multiple times, along the lines of --build-arg or --label.
> 
> Well I already have an approach for that. I am not saying it is best approach but I am working with what I have. Intermediate images (assuming those have both REPOSITORY and TAG set as in `buildah images` output) are storing pullspec of its own base images - I am obtaining this pullspecs with dockerfile-json from Containerfile, doing `buildah inspect` of those intermediate images and mapping pullspecs there and reconstructing the stages by this way (also taking into account that multiple stages can have same base image). Something like argument that would take as parameter stage alias or number and path where builder should be dropped would be definitely better, but I can workaround that if it would be hard to implement.

Would extending `--iidfile`, to be able to have it store the image ID of a particular stage's final image, work here?

> > If the rootfs content was enough...
> 
> Yes the plain rootfs might be a sufficient content for us but from your message I got an impression that `--rm=false`-like argument would be a more feasible approach right?

The `--output` method actually looks simpler to implement, fwiw.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
